payload.answer: AI memory systems require durable source evidence; atomic memory cards must be small and traceable; semantic indices may improve recall but the application database must own lifecycle and conflict state.
citations: AI memory systems need durable source evidence. Atomic memory cards should be small and traceable. A semantic index can improve recall, but the application database must own lifecycle and conflict state.
payload.decision: extract core assertions as atomic memory cards with traceable source evidence

entity_resolution.reason: All entities are explicitly named and unambiguous in context; no resolution needed.

payload.intent: compile atomic memory cards from input about durable memory systems

memory_cards.statement: AI memory systems need durable source evidence
Atomic memory cards should be small and traceable
A semantic index can improve recall
The application database must own lifecycle and conflict state

details: All input assertions extracted as atomic memory cards.
Each card is traceable to the source input.
No superseded or deleted facts presented.
Ambiguity preserved where confidence is not high (e.g., semantic index impact).
Lifecycle and conflict state ownership assigned to application database as mandated.
